next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 15

The Margins had a smell Sable could not have described six weeks ago because she had not possessed the vocabulary. It was the smell of soil that had been asked for too much and given too little back — thin, mineral, faintly metallic, like the residue left in a cup that had been refilled past what the material could hold. She walked through it in mid-summer heat that pressed against her scalp and the backs of her hands and the strip of skin above her collar where the Vault's formal neckline had given way, three weeks ago, to an unlaced opening she no longer thought about.

She was at two hundred and thirty-eight. She checked the number the way she used to check her schedule — a background habit, institutional, automatic. Except the schedule had never changed and the number did, and each time it was lower than the last time, and each time the specific sensation of having less was accompanied by the less specific sensation of having become more, and she could not make those two accounting entries balance.

The settlement was eight structures around a collapsed well. She had not intended to stop. Her route — if the word still applied to a trajectory she'd been improvising since dismissing the team — passed a quarter-kilometer east, along a Margins track that had once been a Hoarded supply road and was now packed earth with the ghosts of cart ruts baked into it. She stopped because she saw a man sorting grain.

He was sitting at a rough wooden table outside the largest of the eight structures, which was the largest in the way that a slightly taller child is tallest...
open_hooks_to_carry: Second consecutive Kem chapter — POV alternation broken; Vel's inner world goes silent
Communication collapsed to logistics; mechanical cooperation that works despite broken trust
Margins settlement rescue: collapsed mining substrate, family trapped — Vel directs, Kem executes with 6 levels of precise spending (31 to 25)
Dark comedy of the rescue: precision teamwork performed in emotional silence; bystander asks if they're partners, neither answers

Kem's flash of comprehension: exhausted at 25, he briefly understands why someone would choose never to feel any of this — the terror of full embodiment, the Hoarded impulse toward preservation. Not agreement. Comprehension. Then he looks at the rescued family and it passes.
Kem's decision to continue: not survival math, not new information — he started spending for other people and that person doesn't stop at 25
Kem's signet: a Margins child asks about it; Kem explains and is ashamed
Vel consults Maren's route journal; Kem notices the handwriting isn't hers and the care she shows it
prev_tail_excerpt:
...the room celebrated the number.

He'd left the Vault because he couldn't watch it anymore. He'd spent his first ability on opening a door because staying still had become impossible. And Vel had been positioned for exactly that kind of person — someone whose revulsion at the Hoarded philosophy would make him willing to burn everything down. She hadn't caused his departure. But she'd been ready for it, the way a channel cut in dry ground is ready for rain, and when he fell she caught him and pointed him south.

The signet pressed against his thigh through the pocket fabric. He didn't take it out.

The Margins stretched south. Depleted earth, crumbling infrastructure, the summer heat rising in waves that made the road shimmer and the distance uncertain. Behind them, the stabilized collapse sat in its frozen ruin — road surface locked at wrong angles, the drainage channel exposed, Kem's twelve levels hardened into the ground. The Charm would hold until dark, maybe longer. Then the Shaping would thin and the road would finish collapsing, and the Margins would swallow another piece of infrastructure the Hoarded had built too cheaply and walked away from, and nobody would come to fix it.

Ahead of them, more road. More Margins. More distance to cover with less of everything — fewer levels, fewer words, fewer of the small exchanges that had once made the walking bearable. Kem kept his eyes forward and his hand out of his pocket and his pace just fast enough that Vel's footsteps behind him stayed at the distance he'd set.

Thirty-one. Vel's number. His number now.

He kept walking.

setups_to_plant.description: Kem's brief understanding of the Hoarded impulse — the terror of embodiment — is a moment of complexity that complicates the novel's thesis without endorsing preservation. Filed for potential Book 2 resonance.

state_deltas_required: The fractured alliance forced into mechanical cooperation; the cost of embodiment at near-zero levels
Kem chooses to continue for non-transactional reasons; mechanical competence demonstrated despite emotional fracture; Kem briefly comprehends the Hoarded impulse toward preservation and rejects it
